confirmed_issues.summary: PUT /api/memories/{id} replaced the photo via the derived delete query deleteByMemoryId with no active transaction (open-in-view is disabled), so photo replacement would fail at runtime; the stale in-memory media collection could also resurrect deleted rows on merge.
confirmed_issues.fix: Made updateMemory @Transactional and switched photo replacement to the entity's cascade + orphanRemoval collection; removed the now-unused MediaRepository.

confirmed_issues.summary: savePhoto stored any uploaded file under /uploads with its original extension; a .html upload would be served same-origin by nginx, a stored-XSS vector with the JWT in localStorage.
confirmed_issues.fix: savePhoto now allows only jpg/jpeg/png/gif/webp extensions and rejects anything else with HTTP 400 and a user-safe Ukrainian message.

confirmed_issues.summary: Deleting a memory (or replacing its photo) removed media rows but left the uploaded file on disk, contradicting the retrofit spec acceptance criterion that delete removes the uploaded file.
confirmed_issues.fix: Added MemoryService.deletePhotoFiles (path-traversal safe, logs and never throws on IO failure) and wired it into deleteMemory and photo replacement.

confirmed_issues.summary: GET /api/memories/{id} re-implemented the private-memory visibility check inline while likes and comments controllers used MemoryService.ensureViewAllowed, risking divergence.

advisories.summary: yearFrom/yearTo are individually bounded (1900-2100) but yearFrom > yearTo is not rejected on either side; accepted as an MVP gap since the spec does not require it.
Photo validation is extension-based; magic-byte sniffing is deferred. Extension allowlisting already prevents the same-origin HTML/SVG serving vector.
